item.title: Public safety will not be compromised despite reduced budget - Tudravu
item.description: Public safety will not be compromised, says Police Commissioner Rusiate Tudravu as the Force prepares to recruit 400 officers desp

item.title: This is not a campaign budget, but a realistic one - Rabuka
item.description: This is not a campaign budget, but a very realistic one. Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka highlighted this to fijvillage News from his residence, saying a campaign budget would have been a much bigger expenditure budget, this is very realistic, as far as the Finance Minister has put it.
item.content:encoded:
This is not a campaign budget, but a very realistic one.

Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka highlighted this to fijvillage News from his residence, saying a campaign budget would have been a much bigger expenditure budget, this is very realistic, as far as the Finance Minister has put it.

Rabuka says this is a very responsible budget for times such as this, and is also to ensure sustainability.

Rabuka also made it clear that they could not afford to give a pay rise to civil servants.

Insert: Rabuka live off the land June 26

PM Rabuka says this is not a campaign budget, people should live off the land

item.title: One more day of Fiji Showcase 2026 before the curtains close
item.description: The final weekend of the entertainment at Fiji Showcase 2026 got off to a lively start tonight, with hundreds of people enjoying a diverse line-up of cultural performances, music and family-friendly entertainment. The evening opened with an electrifying performance by the USP Dance Group, followed by a vibrant cultural showcase from the Rotuma Cultural Performance group, celebrating Fiji's rich cu
item.content:encoded:
The final weekend of the entertainment at Fiji Showcase 2026 got off to a lively start tonight, with hundreds of people enjoying a diverse line-up of cultural performances, music and family-friendly entertainment.

The evening opened with an electrifying performance by the USP Dance Group, followed by a vibrant cultural showcase from the Rotuma Cultural Performance group, celebrating Fiji's rich cultural heritage.

Australian duo AussieBela then thrilled the audience with their breathtaking acrobatic performances.

The crowd responded with plenty of "oohs" and "aahs" as Brazilian juggler PIVA showcased his remarkable talent and precision, captivating people of all ages with his incredible skills.

Music lovers were also kept entertained throughout the evening as DJ Drishant delivered seamless transitions and an energetic mix that kept the atmosphere alive.

Solomon Islands artist Yang Soulja drew a strong crowd with a heartfelt tribute to the late George Fiji Veikoso, performing the beloved classic Lonely Days alongside several of his original songs.

Bringing the night to a memorable close was Mr Sam, who had the audience singing along to his cover of Inner Circle's Sweat (A La La La La Long) before finishing with a selection of his original music.

The entertainment continues tomorrow, the final day of Fiji Showcase 2026, with another packed programme.

Malumu Ni Tobu kei Naivakura will perform from 12pm to 1pm, followed by Back to Front Boys from 1pm to 2pm.

AussieBela will return to the stage from 2pm to 2.30pm before Vude Queen Laisa Vulakoro performs from 2.30pm to 3.30pm.

ManaVa Dance Group will entertain the crowd from 5pm to 5.30pm, followed by Sweet Sensation, whose smooth vocals and energetic band performance will take centrestage from 5.30pm to 6.30pm.

The final performance will feature Liz Vamarasi and Kali Tui from VT1s from 7pm to 8pm, before the gates close at 9pm.

Tomorrow also marks the final opportunity for visitors to experience Fiji Showcase 2026, enjoy the fun rides, sample the wide variety of food on offer and the bargains at more than 100 booths.

Gates at the Vodafone Arena open at 10am.

item.title: 2026/2027 National Budget responsible, well-considered amid global fuel crisis — Prof. Prasad
item.description: The 2026/2027 National Budget is a very responsible and well-thought-out budget in the circumstances that we are in as far as the global fuel crisis goes. National Federation Party Leader and Government MP, Professor Biman Prasad highlighted this today saying the government has ensured that there are no new taxes, that all the good policies that they had to support our people in the last budget is
item.content:encoded:
The 2026/2027 National Budget is a very responsible and well-thought-out budget in the circumstances that we are in as far as the global fuel crisis goes.

National Federation Party Leader and Government MP, Professor Biman Prasad highlighted this today saying the government has ensured that there are no new taxes, that all the good policies that they had to support our people in the last budget is continuing.

Professor Prasad says that he thinks that this budget gives a lot of confidence to the people that there would be no cut in some of those support to cushion the impact of the cost of living.

He says in addition to that, the global environment is changing, with fuel prices likely to come down from July, so that will provide further relief.

The former Finance Minister says given the external circumstances, like the fuel crisis, it's a very responsible budget and continues with the good policies that they had implemented in the last three years.

When asked if he was consulted on this budget, Professor Prasad says they were.

item.title: FRCS did not remove Stop Departure Order on man linked to alleged $20M tax fraud
item.description: As investigations continue into how a naturalized Fijian citizen alleged to have defrauded $20 million in tax left the country despite a Stop Departure Order against him, Fiji Revenue and Customs Service CEO Udit Singh has confirmed that they did not remove the order. While speaking to fijivillage News, Singh says they were alerted of the case as soon as they put the Tax Crimes Task Force into pla
item.content:encoded:
As investigations continue into how a naturalized Fijian citizen alleged to have defrauded $20 million in tax left the country despite a Stop Departure Order against him, Fiji Revenue and Customs Service CEO Udit Singh has confirmed that they did not remove the order.

While speaking to fijivillage News, Singh says they were alerted of the case as soon as they put the Tax Crimes Task Force into place about a month and a half ago.

He says some of these are routine investigations carried out by the compliance and risk teams, which monitor anomalies across the board and follow up on any issues identified.

When questioned if this was before or after the man left the country in April, Singh confirmed he left the country after the Stop Departure Order was issued.

He says they have handed over the file and FICAC and the Police Criminal Investigations Department are looking into it.

While responding to questions by fijivillage News during the post-budget press conference on non-tax compliance, Singh say the assets of some of the organizations are under scrutiny.

item.title: Social welfare recipients to receive new support after 50% increase fuel relief ends next month

item.title: Father of six, shops at multiple supermarkets to stretch family budget as cost of living rises
item.description: A father of six says his family now shops at multiple supermarkets and small shops to find the cheapest prices as the rising cost
